{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/comments/1087674607","pull_request_review_id":1270843079,"id":1087674607,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOABkWpM5A1Jjv","diff_hunk":"@@ -2251,6 +2251,83 @@ instruction, otherwise it'll use a loop.
 """
 replacefield!
 
+"""
+    getglobal(module::Module, name::Symbol, [order::Symbol=:monotonic])
+
+Retrieve the value of the binding `name` from the module `module`. Optionally, an
+ordering can be defined for the operation, otherwise it defaults to monotonic.
+
+While accessing module bindings using [`getfield`](@ref) is still supported to
+maintain compatibility, using `getglobal` should always be preferred since
+`getglobal` allows for control over atomic ordering (`getfield` is always
+monotonic) and better signifies the code's intent both to the user as well as the
+compiler.
+
+Most users should not have to call this function directly -- The
+[`getproperty`](@ref Base.getproperty) function or corresponding syntax (i.e.
+`module.name`) should be preferred in all but few very specific use cases.
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.9"
+    This function requires Julia 1.9 or later.
+
+See also [`getproperty`](@ref Base.getproperty) and [`setglobal!`](@ref).
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> a = 1
+1
+
+julia> module M
+       a = 2
+       end
+Main.M
+
+julia> getglobal(Main, :a)","path":"base/docs/basedocs.jl","position":35,"original_position":35,"commit_id":"f9578fa35fd204cfa0836c3fb15fc9365e266b72","original_commit_id":"f9578fa35fd204cfa0836c3fb15fc9365e266b72","user":{"login":"simeonschaub","id":5220528,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjUyMjA1Mjg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/5220528?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub","html_url":"https://github.com/simeonschaub","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/simeonschaub/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"```suggestion

docstring"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2023-01-25T18:28:47Z","updated_at":"2023-01-26T11:03:46Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/48404","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48404","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48404.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/48404.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"I'm almost certain this came up before, but wasn't able to find the
discussion. I think now that the multidimensional indexing API has
matured a bit, the potential for confusion when allowing iteration is
excedingly low. With this change, it's possible to destructure
`CartesianIndex` objects without having to convert them to a tuple,
which I feel currently makes writing such code unnecessarily annoying.

This also makes `CartesianIndex` broadcast like a scalar which I felt
was consistent with the behavior for objects like `Pair`, but I am open
to other suggestions.

   @ REPL[1]:5

 replacefield!
 
+"""
+    getglobal(module::Module, name::Symbol, [order::Symbol=:monotonic])
+
+Retrieve the value of the binding `name` from the module `module`. Optionally, an
+ordering can be defined for the operation, otherwise it defaults to monotonic.
+
+While accessing module bindings using [`getfield`](@ref) is still supported to
+maintain compatibility, using `getglobal` should always be preferred since
+`getglobal` allows for control over atomic ordering (`getfield` is always
+monotonic) and better signifies the code's intent both to the user as well as the
+compiler.
+
+Most users should not have to call this function directly -- The
+[`getproperty`](@ref Base.getproperty) function or corresponding syntax (i.e.
+`module.name`) should be preferred in all but few very specific use cases.
+
+!!! compat "Julia 1.9"
+    This function requires Julia 1.9 or later.
+
+See also [`getproperty`](@ref Base.getproperty) and [`setglobal!`](@ref).
+
+# Examples
+```jldoctest
+julia> a = 1
+1
+
+julia> module M
+       a = 2
+       end;
+
+julia> getglobal(@__MODULE__, :a)
+1
+
+julia> getglobal(M, :a)
+2
+```
+"""
+getglobal
+
+"""
+    setglobal!(module::Module, name::Symbol, x, [order::Symbol=:monotonic])
+
+Set or change the value of the binding `name` in the module `module` to `x`. No
+type conversion is performed, so if a type has already been declared for the
+binding, `x` must be of appropriate type or an error is thrown.
+
